fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund tracks the Nifty Midcap 150 index, which is a good way to gain exposure to mid-cap companies. It has a low expense ratio and is suitable for long-term growth.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Smallcap 250 Index Fund- Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund tracks the Nifty Smallcap 250 index, which is a good way to gain exposure to small-cap companies. It has a low expense ratio and is suitable for long-term growth.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund tracks the S&P 500 index, which is a good way to gain exposure to international markets. It has a low expense ratio and is suitable for long-term growth.

fund.name: Nippon India Equity Savings Fund - Segregated Portfolio 1 - Direct Plan - Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund is a good way to gain exposure to equity savings. It has a moderate expense ratio and is suitable for long-term growth.
This fund is a good way to gain exposure to aggressive hybrid funds. It has a low expense ratio and is suitable for long-term growth.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Kuvera, Groww, or MFU. Step 2: Start your monthly SIPs in these funds.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: check if your SIPs are running.
After 1 year: see if any fund has fallen behind compared to similar funds.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if your mix has changed a lot. If one category has grown much more than planned, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
plan.rationale: Since you're 30 with 25 years ahead, we can afford to put more in stocks which grow faster over long periods. We've kept 25% in safer options so your money isn't all in one basket. We've chosen funds with low expense ratios to maximise returns over the long term.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily go down. This is normal and recovers over time.
Small-cap and international funds can be more volatile than large-cap funds.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Increase equity to 80% as the portfolio grows
Increase equity to 85% as the portfolio grows
Increase equity to 90% as the portfolio grows
Increase equity to 95% as the portfolio grows
Increase equity to 100% as the portfolio grows

pqs.reasoning: The plan aligns well with Gordon's aggressive risk appetite and long-term horizon, targeting FIRE and a large portfolio. However, it lacks diversification across fund houses and sectors. The risk-return profile is appropriate but could be more aggressive. The plan is internally consistent but doesn't fully leverage tax efficiency for the new_regime investor.
